_principle: Coin presentation always as story. Never as 'you find 14 gold pieces.' Always as what it actually is in the world. The amount tells a story. The condition of the coin tells a story. Where it was hidden tells a story.

none-obvious.description: Nothing obviously valuable but something is there if they look

personal-effects.description: What the person or creature had on them

significant-find.description: Someone was carrying or hiding something of real value

exceptional.description: Rare — only on high-danger encounters or significant locations

narrationExamples.smallHidden: Folded into the lining of his coat — seven Groschen and a Florin, more than a laborer would carry, not enough to explain why he was on this road alone.
narrationExamples.modestLockbox: The lockbox opens to reveal sixty or seventy Pfennig and one Florin with a notch cut in its edge — marked coin, someone's private system for tracking something.
narrationExamples.significantPouch: A leather pouch heavier than it looks. Inside: silver mostly, one or two gold, and a single Mark coin so old the inscription has been worn smooth by handling.

weaponsByCondition.rule: All found weapons and armor have condition state assigned on discovery — rarely pristine, usually Worn or Damaged with specific description of how
